source_documents: cuatro_puertorriqueno.pdf - Puerto Rican Cuatro full plan
metadata.notes: Scale length and fret positions complete. Body dimensions partially extracted - full outline tracing pending in Fusion 360.

name: Cuatro Puertorriqueno
description: National instrument of Puerto Rico. Evolved over centuries from a 4-string instrument to 10 strings in 5 courses tuned in fourths with octave and unison pairs. Body shape is viola-like with dramatic outward-flaring bouts and decorative horn extensions at lower bout corners. Entirely distinct from Venezuelan cuatro despite the shared name. Declared national instrument of Puerto Rico.
cam_notes: Pending body generator. Unique viola-like body shape with horn extensions requires dedicated outline tracing in Fusion 360.

strings.pairing_notes: Lower courses paired at octave, upper courses paired at unison
strings.tuning_notes: Tuned in fourths from lowest to highest course: B E A D G

scale.fret_positions_notes: Values shown are fret interval widths in inches, not cumulative distances

body.body_shape_description: Dramatic outward-flaring upper and lower bouts with pronounced waist. Decorative pointed horn extensions project outward from lower bout corners. Tail has decorative heart-shaped ornament. Entirely unlike the guitar-shaped Venezuelan cuatro.
body.lower_bout_width_source: 12 inches annotated at bottom of front view
body.body_depth_source: 3 inches annotated at left side view

soundhole.notes: Single round soundhole positioned in upper section of body

distinctive_features: Viola-like body outline - not guitar shaped
Decorative outward horn extensions at lower bout corners
Extended decorative tailpiece with leaf motif
Upper body has rectangular inlay panel
Lower bout has rectangular bridge plate
body.construction_source: Material legend at bottom of drawing - spruce top, poplar and mahogany layers

neck.headstock_notes: Elaborate carved headstock with floral and leaf decoration. Significantly more ornate than Venezuelan cuatro. Traditional Puerto Rican design.
neck.joint_notes: Standard acoustic neck joint visible in side section view

variants.name: Traditional
variants.description: Traditional 10-string 5-course Puerto Rican cuatro. Elaborate carved headstock and decorative tailpiece.
variants.data_notes: Scale length and fret positions complete. Body dimensions partially extracted - full outline tracing required in Fusion 360 to capture viola-like body shape with horn extensions.
